intelligence.hypothesis: Telemetry stable; no immediate stress signal.

vision_report.model: The audit followed a rigorous maker-checker mechanism. First, an initial scan of the expected biome registry and all images was performed to establish a baseline and identify key components. This 'maker' phase created an initial checklist. Subsequently, a 'checker' phase involved a meticulous, chronological image-by-image audit. Each image was compared to its predecessor, identifying specific changes in plant morphology, health indicators, and biome anomalies. This iterative comparison allowed for validation of observations and precise tracking of temporal deltas, culminating in a comprehensive health inference based solely on visual evidence.

P2_mexican_mint.reconciliation: Matches registry from [EARLIEST] to [T-1]. Visually lost in [CURRENT] due to darkness.

unmonitored_money_plant.reconciliation: Matches registry from [EARLIEST] to [T-1]. Visually lost in [CURRENT] due to darkness.

inventory_reconciliation.systemic_loss: None confirmed for the plants themselves; however, a 'Systemic Observational Loss' occurred in the [CURRENT] image due to extreme darkness, rendering all registered plants unobservable.

P2_mexican_mint.description: A small plant with approximately 4-5 rounded, turgid, medium-green leaves. Appears robust. Electronic sensors (white connector, purple PCB) are clearly visible in the soil.
P2_mexican_mint.health_inference: Good health, leaves are firm and well-hydrated.

unmonitored_money_plant.description: A single cutting with one prominent, turgid, dark-green leaf. The stem is submerged in clear water within a white cup.
unmonitored_money_plant.health_inference: Good health, leaf is firm and vibrant.

P2_mexican_mint.description: Similar leaf count and turgidity as [EARLIEST]. Leaves appear slightly less vibrant green, possibly due to minor lighting variation, but still generally healthy. Sensors and soil condition unchanged.

unmonitored_money_plant.description: Unchanged from [EARLIEST]. Leaf remains turgid and green. Water level consistent.

P2_mexican_mint.description: No significant change from [T-3]. Leaves maintain their form and color. Plant appears stable.

unmonitored_money_plant.description: No significant change from [T-3]. Leaf and water level consistent. Cutting remains stable.

P2_mexican_mint.description: No significant change from [T-2]. Plant continues to appear stable.

unmonitored_money_plant.description: No significant change from [T-2]. Cutting remains stable.

P2_mexican_mint.health_inference: Cannot be inferred due to complete lack of visual data. Visually lost.

unmonitored_money_plant.description: Not visually discernible due to extreme darkness. Only a very faint, dark outline of the cup is visible.
unmonitored_money_plant.health_inference: Cannot be inferred due to complete lack of visual data. Visually lost.

soil_black_pot.earliest_to_T-1: Appears consistently somewhat dry on the surface, but not severely cracked. No incidental growth, moss, or fungal presence observed.
soil_black_pot.current: Not visually discernible due to darkness.

water_white_cup.earliest_to_T-1: Appears clear with a consistent level. No algae or debris visible.
water_white_cup.current: Not visually discernible due to darkness.

desk_surface.earliest_to_T-1: Dark, featureless surface. No debris or anomalies observed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-3: Minimal change. Slight perceived reduction in vibrancy of Mexican Mint leaves, likely a lighting artifact rather than a health decline. Money Plant unchanged.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: No discernible change in either plant or biome.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: No discernible change in either plant or biome.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_current: Catastrophic loss of visual information. The entire scene is plunged into extreme darkness, rendering all plant and biome details unobservable. This represents an 'Observational System Failure'.

visual_health_inference.earliest_to_T-1: Both the Mexican Mint and Money Plant cutting appear to be in good, stable health. Leaves are turgid and green, with no signs of wilting, discoloration, or pest damage.
visual_health_inference.current: Health cannot be inferred due to complete lack of visual data. The plants are visually 'lost'.

anomalies.observational_system_failure: The most significant anomaly is the extreme darkness in the [CURRENT] image, which prevents any visual assessment of the plants or biome. This is an external factor affecting observation, not necessarily an internal plant anomaly.

vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical observation sequence reveals a period of stable plant health for both the Mexican Mint (P2) and the Money Plant cutting from the earliest image up to T-1. Both plants exhibited turgid, green foliage with no visible signs of stress, disease, or significant growth/decline. The soil in the black pot remained consistently somewhat dry on the surface, and the water in the white cup remained clear. However, the final image, labeled [CURRENT], presents a critical observational anomaly: the entire scene is plunged into extreme darkness, rendering all plant material and biome details completely indiscernible. While the physical presence of the pots is faintly outlined, no health inference can be made for the plants in this final state. This represents a systemic failure of the observation system rather than a confirmed decline in plant health.
vision_report.confidence: High, for observations and inferences from [EARLIEST] to [T-1]. For [CURRENT], confidence in the *lack of observable data* is high, but confidence in the *actual plant state* is null due to data unavailability.
